encounter.reason: Intensive outpatient program completion, sustained sobriety achieved
encounter.location: Bridgetown Recovery Center, 920 SE Sandy Blvd, Portland, OR 97214

screening.name: AUDIT (Alcohol Use Disorders Identification Test) score
screening.interpretation: low risk (markedly improved from 28 at intake)

screening.name: PHQ-9 (Patient Health Questionnaire-9) depression screening score

conditions.name: alcohol use disorder, moderate — in early remission
conditions.note: Updated to reflect early remission — patient achieved sustained sobriety (sobriety date April 14, 2023), AUDIT score decreased from 28 to 4, liver enzymes fully normalized, IOP program completed.

conditions.name: depressive symptoms, mild
conditions.note: PHQ-9 of 8 indicating mild depression, likely related to bipolar II depressive component and psychosocial adjustment in recovery. To be monitored.

medications.name: naltrexone 50 mg oral tablet
medications.sig: Take 50 mg by mouth once daily, indefinitely
medications.reason: alcohol use disorder — craving suppression and relapse prevention
medications.note: Continued indefinitely at this encounter. Patient tolerating well with no adverse effects.

medications.name: thiamine 100 mg oral tablet
medications.sig: Take 100 mg by mouth once daily
medications.reason: nutritional supplementation during alcohol use disorder treatment
medications.note: Discontinued at this encounter. No longer clinically indicated given normalized liver function and adequate nutritional status.

medications.name: lamotrigine 200 mg oral tablet
medications.sig: Take 200 mg by mouth once daily

medications.name: quetiapine 50 mg oral tablet
medications.sig: Take 50 mg by mouth at bedtime
medications.reason: bipolar II disorder (sleep and mood stabilization)

document_references.description: Documents successful completion of the 12-week intensive outpatient program for alcohol use disorder (moderate). Summarizes program participation (~36 group sessions, 12 individual counseling sessions), achievement of sobriety on April 14, 2023, improvement in AUDIT score from 28 to 4, PHQ-9 improvement to 8, and liver enzyme normalization. Includes individualized aftercare plan: continue naltrexone indefinitely, attend AA meetings at least twice weekly, follow-up in 3 months.

Office visit note for April 24, 2023 IOP completion visit with Dr. Rosa Delgado. Documents sustained sobriety, vitals, screening scores (AUDIT 4, PHQ-9 8), lab results showing normalized liver enzymes, assessment of AUD in early remission with IOP completed, and plan for ongoing naltrexone, thiamine discontinuation, AA attendance, and 3-month aftercare follow-up.

Letter sent to Dr. James Okafor at Portland Behavioral Health Associates documenting IOP completion, sustained sobriety, normalized lab results, and ongoing naltrexone plan. Requests Dr. Okafor continue management of bipolar II disorder and monitor for mood changes in early recovery.

diagnostic_reports.type: comprehensive metabolic panel (CMP) with liver enzymes
diagnostic_reports.description: CMP drawn at this visit. CMP within normal limits; liver enzymes fully normalized from elevated values at intake. Only AST, ALT, and GGT individually reported — remaining CMP components not individually enumerated.
